Class Event

java.lang.Object
it.polimi.Abstract.Card
it.polimi.Game.Elements.Tribe
it.polimi.Abstract.Event
Direct Known Subclasses:
Hunting, RockPaintings, ShamanicRitual, Sustenance
public abstract class Event extends Tribe
Base class for all event cards in the game. Events are a subset of Tribe cards that trigger special effects during the game rounds.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Event(Era era, Position position, EventType type)
    Creates an event card with the given era, position, and event type.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    abstract void
    applyEffect(EventContext context)
    Applies the effect of the event to the provided context.
    void
    assignToPlayer(Player player)
    Events are not assigned to players directly, so this method does nothing.
    EventType
    getType()
    Returns the type of the event.
    boolean
    isEvent()
    Events are always marked as event cards.

  • Constructor Details

    • Event

      public Event(Era era, Position position, EventType type)
      Creates an event card with the given era, position, and event type.
      Parameters:
      era - The era this event belongs to.
      position - The initial board position.
      type - The event's type.

  • Method Details

    • getType

      public EventType getType()
      Returns the type of the event.
      Returns:
      The EventType enum value.

    • isEvent

      public boolean isEvent()
      Events are always marked as event cards.
      Specified by:
      isEvent in class Tribe
      Returns:
      Always true.

    • assignToPlayer

      public void assignToPlayer(Player player)
      Events are not assigned to players directly, so this method does nothing.
      Specified by:
      assignToPlayer in class Tribe
      Parameters:
      player - The player (ignored).

    • applyEffect

      public abstract void applyEffect(EventContext context)
      Applies the effect of the event to the provided context. Subclasses must implement specific effect logic.
      Parameters:
      context - The context containing players and other data needed for resolution.
